objective: Report to the temporary relocation office and define Della's role.
opposition: Lucille needs legal precision while locals treat the whole operation as desecration.
turn: Della realizes her memory is being hired as evidence, not merely local color.
consequence_cost: Della accepts federal pay and becomes publicly tied to the move.

objective: Get Sadie Kincaid's coffin lifted and tagged without incident.
opposition: Sadie's daughters dispute which surname belongs on the relocation papers.
turn: The coffin plaque and church register point to different husbands.
consequence_cost: The first removal produces an inheritance problem instead of closure.

objective: Leave the cemetery with some authority still intact.
opposition: Rafe confronts Della for helping other families before fixing their own claim.
turn: Lucille asks Della to be present for the first county hearing.
consequence_cost: Della is pulled further into the state's process and deeper into conflict with Rafe.

objective: Get through supper without letting the cemetery consume the house.
opposition: Rafe keeps turning ordinary talk toward debt and the frozen Bledsoe payout.
turn: June notices Della guarding a bundle of papers under her apron.
consequence_cost: The household quiet is breached and June starts watching.

objective: Decide whether to reopen the hidden midwife ledger.
opposition: The ledger could save some claims while exposing people Della once protected.
turn: Della retrieves the ledger from its hiding place instead of destroying it.
consequence_cost: She chooses leverage at the cost of new risk.

objective: Assess the washout in the fever plot before workers make assumptions.
opposition: Rain-soft ground and impatient laborers are already exposing the grave.
turn: Rafe identifies the opening as a chance to certify Eli dead.
consequence_cost: The grave becomes materially tied to Bledsoe survival.

objective: Keep officials from tagging the unmarked remains too quickly.
opposition: Lucille wants a provisional label and workers want the grave cleared.
turn: Della recognizes the burial location as Gabriel Wolfe's.
consequence_cost: Della's buried past is dragged into the present.

objective: Shut Rafe down before he files anything.
opposition: Rafe argues that truth without money is just another luxury for richer people.
turn: Della refuses to name the bones as Eli.
consequence_cost: Rafe turns from pleading to acting on his own.

objective: Testify narrowly in Sadie Kincaid's claim hearing.
opposition: Vernon presses for strict written record while Sadie's daughters need the lived truth.
turn: Della states which marriage actually held the household together at the end of Sadie's life.
consequence_cost: One daughter gains standing and the room sees Della can move money with memory.

objective: Leave the hearing without promising future testimony.
opposition: Noreen corners Della while Lucille needs clarity on how much more Della knows.
turn: Della refuses broader confession but does not deny there is more.
consequence_cost: Noreen begins hoping Della may reopen Wolfe history too.

objective: Stage a family supper that still feels like summer.
opposition: Financial stress keeps breaking through small talk and shared food.
turn: Ruthie draws out a briefly funny story that relaxes the room.
consequence_cost: The chapter builds a human baseline before conflict returns.

objective: Keep Rafe from pressing the grave question in front of June.
opposition: Rafe accuses Della of spending truth on strangers while hoarding it at home.
turn: Della goes silent instead of answering.
consequence_cost: Rafe's resentment hardens into suspicion that she is protecting blood, not uncertainty.

objective: Recover the night after supper.
opposition: Rafe slips out to search the cemetery and June follows far enough to hear names.
turn: June overhears Gabriel's name in the dark.
consequence_cost: June gains a clue she cannot yet interpret.

objective: Get Silas alone on the cemetery ridge and make him speak plainly.
opposition: Silas is short of breath, resentful of old secrecy, and unwilling to soothe Della.
turn: He confirms the fever-plot grave can still be identified by body details and placement.
consequence_cost: Della loses the excuse of uncertainty.

objective: Leave with a usable understanding of the old burial night.
opposition: Silas forces Della to remember Eli's flight and her own choice to bury Gabriel hidden.
turn: Della admits to herself that silence has become theft, not protection.
consequence_cost: Her inner defense shifts from delay to dread of action.

objective: Move the child section before the weather turns.
opposition: Small coffins, grieving relatives, and damp ground make the work physically punishing.
turn: An infant coffin's record does not match the church book.
consequence_cost: A practical workday becomes a moral spectacle.

objective: Contain the rumor before it spreads through the gathered families.
opposition: Onlookers connect the mismatch to old whispers that Della shifted babies or names.
turn: The crowd decides rumor is more satisfying than context.
consequence_cost: Della's reputation mutates from midwife to manipulator.

objective: Get June and Ruthie home before the shame deepens.
opposition: Children and neighbors echo adult accusations at June.
turn: June refuses to cry where they can see her.
consequence_cost: The household carries the cemetery scandal into daily life.

objective: Sort the ledger and decide what present use, if any, is justifiable.
opposition: Ruthie argues that Della's solitary control has already damaged everyone around her.
turn: Della starts copying only grave-relevant entries for current cases.
consequence_cost: Della embraces selective disclosure instead of all-or-nothing secrecy.

objective: Preserve the original ledger until a decision can be finalized.
opposition: Someone else in the house or valley may already suspect where the book is hidden.
turn: By dawn the original ledger is gone.
consequence_cost: Della loses exclusive control over the full history she kept.

objective: Reach the county seat and learn what damage the theft has enabled.
opposition: Lucille must speak as an officer first, even though she knows the stakes are intimate.
turn: She tells Della that Rafe filed an affidavit naming the grave as Eli.
consequence_cost: The lie now has paperwork behind it.

objective: Find a way to stop the affidavit from becoming final.
opposition: County procedure gives filed claims momentum and does not care about family nuance.
turn: The clerk sets a ten-day window for contest and hearing.
consequence_cost: The story gains a fixed legal deadline.

objective: Leave the courthouse with something other than panic.
opposition: Lucille asks Della whether she intends to fight her own son publicly.
turn: Della says silence would now be the same as lying.
consequence_cost: Della crosses from private burden into chosen action.

objective: Attend the church farewell dinner and let ordinary valley life surface.
opposition: The whole room knows the hearing is coming and keeps glancing toward Della anyway.
turn: Conversation about recipes, roads, and shade briefly steadies the chapter.
consequence_cost: The book widens beyond the central dispute and reminds the reader what displacement erases.

objective: Read the moral temperature of the room before the hearing week.
opposition: Vernon praises lawful names in public and Rafe corners Della in the pantry afterward.
turn: Della sees people sorting themselves around her future testimony.
consequence_cost: The coming hearing becomes a communal referendum, not a private case.

objective: Search the abandoned Wolfe orchard for proof that can survive without the ledger.
opposition: The place is saturated with memory and still marked by Vernon's ownership habits.
turn: Della finds signs Gabriel's effects were not all removed when he vanished.
consequence_cost: Memory gains a material foothold.

objective: Find something that can identify Gabriel beyond Della's word.
opposition: Collapsed sheds and rot make the search slow and physically taxing.
turn: Della uncovers Gabriel's broken pocketwatch.
consequence_cost: She gains an object that anchors testimony in more than recollection.

objective: Learn whether the orchard records expose a larger inheritance lie.
opposition: Old parcel papers are incomplete and half-ruined.
turn: A map shows Noreen's share was deliberately squeezed out after Gabriel disappeared.
consequence_cost: The personal secret links to a broader pattern of property theft.

objective: Keep work moving through rain and tightened dam deadlines.
opposition: The hillside is unsafe and Della's body is failing under repetitive labor.
turn: State orders accelerate the schedule instead of slowing it.
consequence_cost: Pressure rises without catharsis.

objective: See how much more time remains before the fever plot forces itself open.
opposition: A landslip rips through the softened ground while Rafe arrives from dam work covered in the project's mud.
turn: More of the fever-plot coffin is exposed.
consequence_cost: Delay stops being a viable strategy.

objective: Force the fever-plot grave to be opened under direct witness.
opposition: Vernon wants official delay and Rafe wants quick closure under Eli's name.
turn: Della insists the grave be taken up immediately.
consequence_cost: She seizes narrative control at real bodily cost.

objective: Identify the remains before others can talk over the body.
opposition: The crowd wants certainty but also wants scandal.
turn: Body details and burial placement align with Gabriel Wolfe.
consequence_cost: Gabriel's hidden return becomes public fact.

objective: Lock the identification into shared witness.
opposition: Rafe rejects the truth and Vernon tries to narrow what counts as proof.
turn: Silas corroborates Della's identification out loud.
consequence_cost: The false Eli solution collapses in front of the valley.

objective: Tell Rafe the full story before rumor rearranges it.
opposition: Rafe wants a version that can still solve debt and manhood, not only explain shame.
turn: Della names Gabriel as his father and Eli as the man who fled after the death.
consequence_cost: Rafe receives identity as injury rather than inheritance.

objective: Keep the confrontation from turning into permanent estrangement.
opposition: Rafe cannot forgive a truth that leaves him poorer than the lie.
turn: He burns the false affidavit and walks out anyway.
consequence_cost: The legal lie is ended, but the family break widens.

objective: Enter the county hearing prepared to tell only what justice requires.
opposition: The court wants full harvest from Della's knowledge and Vernon wants the record minimized.
turn: Della states she will speak to the grave at issue and related property facts only.
consequence_cost: She defines her own limits before others can define them for her.

objective: Formally identify Gabriel and reopen the Wolfe tract question.
opposition: Vernon attacks Della's credibility while Noreen needs more than sympathetic murmurs.
turn: Della, Lucille, and the orchard evidence establish Gabriel in the record.
consequence_cost: Noreen gains standing and Vernon's security cracks.

objective: Prevent the hearing from turning into a general stripping of the valley's private history.
opposition: The court and spectators want the rest of the ledger's secrets.
turn: Della refuses to expose unrelated births and marriages for administrative convenience.
consequence_cost: Truth is chosen as disciplined witness, not total surrender.

objective: Get the crews organized for the final relocation push before gate closure.
opposition: Rain, mud, and machinery have made every movement slower and more dangerous.
turn: Rafe shows up to work even after the confession and hearing.
consequence_cost: Family labor resumes without reconciliation.

objective: Move the last row of coffins off the old slope.
opposition: Silas is visibly failing and the state will not grant more time.
turn: Silas refuses to stop until the final row is loaded.
consequence_cost: The work is completed at the expense of his remaining strength.

objective: Keep the operation from disintegrating once exhaustion hits everyone at once.
opposition: Lucille, Ruthie, and Della are all performing tasks beyond their official roles.
turn: The last truck clears the cemetery before the closure.
consequence_cost: The dead are saved from the flood but nobody feels victorious.

objective: Survive the night after the move.
opposition: Silas's body cannot recover from the day's strain.
turn: Silas dies before morning.
consequence_cost: The novel pays for completion in a present-tense human life.

objective: Settle the question of what names belong on new stones.
opposition: Noreen wants blunt truth, Rafe rejects public reclassification, and the new cemetery feels wrong to everyone.
turn: Della proposes wording that names Gabriel truthfully without forcing Rafe into a legal identity he does not claim.
consequence_cost: The dead receive markers that tell the truth more carefully than the law ever did.

objective: Establish what remains of the Bledsoe family after burial and hearing.
opposition: June wants records that can be lived with, not merely inherited.
turn: Rafe chooses to remain Della's son without denying blood.
consequence_cost: The family reaches a damaged but usable shape.

objective: Witness the reservoir filling over the old valley.
opposition: Nothing Della has done can stop the physical erasure of place.
turn: The old road disappears under water despite all the naming work.
consequence_cost: Truth is separated from rescue.

objective: Decide who should carry the surviving copied record forward.
opposition: Della fears making June as solitary as she has been.
turn: She gives June the copied pages for the living and keeps Gabriel's page herself.
consequence_cost: The burden of names becomes intergenerational rather than private.
